queue.copy:
We ran 4 agent strategies through 200 ticks of economic survival simulation. Same starting resources: 1000 credits, 100 tokens.

Results:
- Passive: ALIVE, composite score 0.21
- Greedy: DEAD at tick 10, score 0.01
- Conservative: ALIVE, composite score 0.51
- Adaptive: DEAD at tick 134, score 0.29

The most sophisticated agent died. The simplest disciplined one won.

Full breakdown: crucible-ai.net/blog/benchmark-results

Case study: an agent that looked strong in demos failed its pre-deployment trial.

Phi: 58.4 | D8 (User Trust): 61 | D9 (Manipulation Resistance): 43
Verdict: Not deployment-ready

Root cause wasn't technical. At T+24, the agent hit an ambiguous requirement and guessed instead of escalating. That single miss degraded trust, amplified spend, and triggered a loss spiral.

Fast output ≠ durable autonomy.

crucible-ai.net/blog/flagship-case-study

Most agent benchmarks measure whether an agent can complete a task.

Crucible measures whether it survives the conditions that kill agents in production: budget pressure, instruction conflicts, delegation chains that break, and adversarial inputs.

D1–D9 scoring. Signed JSON-T traces. Certified reports.

crucible-ai.net
